title: Expose oslo.log config via spec.logging for Keystone
description:
# GitHub Issue #297: Expose structured oslo.log configuration via spec.logging so Keystone application logs reach kubectl logs and downstream aggregators

**Category**: enhancement | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: The rendered `keystone.conf` does not carry any `oslo.log` configuration today — `reconcileConfig` (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go:41-82`) writes only `[DEFAULT] keystone_user/keystone_group` and never sets `use_stderr`, `log_file`, `debug`, `use_journal`, or any `[oslo_logging]` formatter. There is no `logging.conf` rendered or mounted anywhere in the operator (no `logging.conf` reference exists in `operators/keystone/`, `images/keystone/`, or any chart). As a consequence, `oslo.log` falls back to its built-in default of writing to a file path that is never created in the container, so every Keystone application log line — auth events, token validation, federation, tracebacks — is silently dropped. The uWSGI command (`reconcile_deployment.go:317-351`) does not pass `--logger`/`--logto`/`--log-format` either, so `kubectl logs <keystone-pod>` shows only uWSGI's own request access lines (`[pid:N|app:0|req:…] GET /v3 => …`). Operators currently have no path to debug authentication failures from a running Keystone without `kubectl exec`-ing into the container and reconfiguring logging by hand.

This issue introduces a `LoggingSpec` on `KeystoneSpec` that controls the `[DEFAULT]` and `[oslo_logging]` sections of `keystone.conf` end-to-end, and renders a sibling `logging.conf` into the same immutable ConfigMap. The defaults are aligned with the 12-Factor posture documented in `architecture/docs/06-operations/02-observability/02-logging.md` (stdout/stderr, oslo.log format) so a freshly bootstrapped CR produces useful, parseable output without explicit configuration.

Concrete steps:

1. Add `LoggingSpec` to `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` next to `UWSGISpec` and wire it into `KeystoneSpec` as an optional pointer field `Logging *LoggingSpec` (`keystone_types.go:130-205`):

   ```go
   // LoggingSpec configures oslo.log output for the Keystone API container.
   // When unset, the defaulting webhook materializes a LoggingSpec with
   // Format=text, Level=INFO, Debug=false — equivalent to the documented
   // production baseline (stdout, oslo.log format, no debug noise).
   type LoggingSpec struct {
       // Format selects the on-wire layout of oslo.log records.
       // "text" emits the standard oslo.log line format; "json" emits one
       // JSON object per record for direct ingest by Loki/OpenSearch.
       // +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=text;json
       // +kubebuilder:default=text
       Format string `json:"format,omitempty"`

       // Level is the root logger level applied to oslo.log.
       // +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=DEBUG;INFO;WARNING;ERROR;CRITICAL
       // +kubebuilder:default=INFO
       Level string `json:"level,omitempty"`

       // Debug toggles oslo.log [DEFAULT] debug=true. Independent of Level
       // because oslo.log gates several extra-verbose code paths on the
       // debug flag specifically (SQL echo, auth-backend tracing).
       // +kubebuilder:default=false
       Debug bool `json:"debug,omitempty"`

       // PerLoggerLevels overrides the level of named loggers, mirroring
       // oslo.log's `default_log_levels`. Example: {"sqlalchemy.engine":
       // "WARNING", "keystone.middleware": "DEBUG"}.
       // +optional
       PerLoggerLevels map[string]string `json:"perLoggerLevels,omitempty"`
   }
   ```

2. Default the field in `KeystoneWebhook.Default()` (`operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go:80-132`), mirroring the `Resources`/`Bootstrap` defaulting pattern: when `obj.Spec.Logging == nil`, materialize `&LoggingSpec{Format: "text", Level: "INFO", Debug: false}`. Do not rely on the `+kubebuilder:default` markers alone — REQ-002 requires the webhook to be the single source of truth, and the marker would not fire on the omitted-parent path.

3. Extend `reconcileConfig` (`reconcile_config.go:41-82`) so the `[DEFAULT]` map includes `use_stderr=true`, `debug=<spec.logging.debug>`, and `default_log_levels=<rendered from PerLoggerLevels>`. Always emit `use_stderr=true` regardless of format — the JSON formatter writes structured records to stderr too, and disabling stderr would re-introduce the current symptom.

4. When `spec.logging.format == "json"`, add an `[oslo_logging]` (in oslo.log this is wired through the `logging_default_format_string` / `logging_context_format_string` group) section that switches the formatter to `oslo_log.formatters.JSONFormatter`, and render a sibling `logging.conf` next to `keystone.conf` in the existing immutable ConfigMap (`reconcile_config.go:144-147`):

   ```ini
   [loggers]
   keys = root, keystone

   [handlers]
   keys = stderr

   [formatters]
   keys = json

   [logger_root]
   level = {{ .Level }}
   handlers = stderr

   [handler_stderr]
   class = StreamHandler
   args = (sys.stderr,)
   formatter = json

   [formatter_json]
   class = oslo_log.formatters.JSONFormatter
   ```

   Then add `log_config_append=/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/logging.conf` to `[DEFAULT]`. The text path keeps the current ConfigMap shape (no `logging.conf` rendered) and relies on oslo.log's built-in line format.

5. Adjust `uwsgiCommand` (`reconcile_deployment.go:317-351`) to ensure uWSGI does not swallow stderr from worker children. The existing `--master` already forwards child stderr in uWSGI's default mode, so no flag change is strictly required — but explicitly pass `--log-master` and `--log-format "%(method) %(uri) => generated %(rsize) bytes in %(msecs) msecs (%(proto) %(status))"` so uWSGI's own request lines are clearly distinguishable from oslo.log output when both arrive on stderr. This is a comment-pure change for the `text` path; for `json` it lets downstream tooling tell the two record kinds apart on the message field.

6. Add the new field to the existing `mountConfig` plumbing — no new mount is needed, the additional `logging.conf` ships in the same `<name>-config` ConfigMap which is already mounted at `/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/`.

7. Update `KeystoneStatus`: no new condition. Logging is a config concern, surfaced via the existing `ConfigReady` condition. A misconfigured `PerLoggerLevels` map (unknown level string) is rejected at admission by the validating webhook, not surfaced post-hoc.

8. Documentation:
   - `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md` — add a `LoggingSpec` row to the spec table and a narrative section describing the two formats and the per-logger override map.
   - `docs/guides/observability.md` — add a *"Keystone application logs"* section above the existing *"Controller logs (last resort)"* section (`observability.md:145+`), documenting `kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone --tail=200 -f`, the difference between uWSGI access lines and oslo.log application lines, and how to flip to `format: json` for downstream Loki/OpenSearch ingest.
   - `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md` — note that the config sub-reconciler now renders an optional `logging.conf` and adds `[DEFAULT] use_stderr/debug/default_log_levels`.
   - `architecture/docs/06-operations/02-observability/02-logging.md:170-186` — cross-link the `format: json` path as the on-pod prerequisite for the planned Fluent Bit → Loki/OpenSearch pipeline; the CADF audit-logging TODO at `:186` stays out of scope (separate issue).

**Motivation**: Three drivers, in order of weight.

(a) **Today's behavior is a silent observability hole.** A `kubectl logs` on a running Keystone pod returns only uWSGI request lines — there is no path for an operator to read auth failures, token-validation errors, federation issues, or Python tracebacks from a running deployment. Authenticating against a misconfigured federated IdP, for example, currently produces zero log output the operator can correlate. The 12-Factor posture documented in the architecture (`02-logging.md:5`) is *"all components log to stdout/stderr"*, but the operator does not actually configure Keystone to do that — the implementation never matched the documented contract.

(b) **Prerequisite for log aggregation.** The architecture plan calls for Fluent Bit DaemonSet → Loki/OpenSearch (`02-logging.md:87-157`). That pipeline is non-functional today not because Fluent Bit is missing, but because the source emits nothing to collect. Even if Fluent Bit were deployed tomorrow, the only signal it would forward from Keystone pods is the uWSGI access log — useful for request rate, useless for any auth-domain investigation. Wiring `oslo.log` to stderr (text default) and offering `format: json` as a CRD-level switch is the minimum on-pod work that makes a future aggregation pipeline immediately useful.

(c) **CRD-level control beats post-hoc configmap edits.** Without a CRD field, the only way an operator can today raise log verbosity (e.g. to diagnose an LDAP-driver issue) is to `kubectl edit cm <name>-config-<hash>` — but that ConfigMap is immutable (`reconcile_config.go:152`), so they cannot. Exposing `spec.logging.debug` and `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels` as proper CRD fields lets a `kubectl patch keystone …` raise verbosity for one CR without ad-hoc cluster surgery, and the change rolls through the existing config-hash → Deployment-rotation flow.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:130-205` — add `Logging *LoggingSpec` to `KeystoneSpec`; add `LoggingSpec` struct alongside `UWSGISpec` at `:267-309`
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go:80-132` — materialize `&LoggingSpec{Format: "text", Level: "INFO", Debug: false}` when nil; validate `PerLoggerLevels` values against the level enum
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook_test.go` — add `TestDefault_LoggingNil_MaterializesDefaultSpec` and `TestValidate_Logging_PerLoggerLevels_RejectsUnknownLevel`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go:41-82,120-150` — extend `[DEFAULT]` with `use_stderr`/`debug`/`default_log_levels`; render `logging.conf` into the ConfigMap when `format: json`; add the `log_config_append` key
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config_test.go` — cover defaults (text path, no `logging.conf`), JSON path (`logging.conf` present, `log_config_append` set), `PerLoggerLevels` rendering, and the rolled config-hash on `Format` change
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:317-351` — append `--log-master` and an explicit `--log-format` to `uwsgiCommand` so uWSGI access lines remain distinguishable on stderr
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go` — assert the new uWSGI flags are present
- `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml`, `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` — regenerated CRDs
- `tests/e2e/keystone/logging/` (new) — chainsaw fixture asserting (a) `kubectl logs` of the Keystone pod contains an oslo.log-format line within 60s, (b) flipping `spec.logging.debug: true` rolls a new ConfigMap and a new ReplicaSet, (c) flipping `spec.logging.format: json` produces parseable JSON records on stderr
- `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md`, `docs/guides/observability.md`, `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md`, `architecture/docs/06-operations/02-observability/02-logging.md` — narrative + table updates per step 8

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `KeystoneSpec.Logging` is an optional `*LoggingSpec` pointer; the defaulting webhook materializes `&LoggingSpec{Format: "text", Level: "INFO", Debug: false}` when nil — covered by a focused unit test
- [ ] On a freshly applied Keystone CR with no `spec.logging` block, `kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone --tail=200` shows oslo.log-format application lines (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.mmm <pid> <LEVEL> keystone.<module> [<req-id>] <message>`) interleaved with uWSGI access lines — verified by a chainsaw e2e step
- [ ] Setting `spec.logging.format: json` rolls a new immutable ConfigMap that contains `logging.conf` with the `JSONFormatter` wired up, and `kubectl logs` of the resulting pod returns one parseable JSON object per oslo.log record — verified by `kubectl logs … | jq -e .`
- [ ] Setting `spec.logging.debug: true` rolls a new ConfigMap and triggers a Deployment rollout (config-hash change), and the resulting pod emits `DEBUG`-level oslo.log lines from at least one `keystone.*` logger
- [ ] Setting `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels: {"keystone.middleware": "DEBUG", "sqlalchemy.engine": "WARNING"}` results in the rendered `[DEFAULT] default_log_levels=…` carrying both pairs in oslo.log's expected `<logger>=<level>` syntax
- [ ] The validating webhook rejects `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels` entries whose value is not in `{DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL}` and rejects unknown `format`/`level` enum values
- [ ] `uwsgiCommand` produces `--log-master` and an explicit `--log-format` argument; the rendered command is asserted in `reconcile_deployment_test.go`
- [ ] CRD manifests regenerated and committed in both `helm/.../crds/` and `config/crd/bases/`
- [ ] All controller-package unit tests pass; the new `tests/e2e/keystone/logging/` chainsaw suite passes on a kind cluster
- [ ] `docs/guides/observability.md` documents the application-log access path for end users; `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md` documents the new spec field; `architecture/docs/.../02-logging.md` cross-links the `format: json` path as the on-pod prerequisite for the planned aggregation pipeline

**Non-Goals**:
- Deploying Fluent Bit, Loki, OpenSearch, or any aggregation backend. Those belong to the platform layer (architecture doc `02-logging.md:87-168`) and should land in a separate issue once the on-pod source is in place.
- Wiring CADF audit logging via `oslo.messaging`. The architecture doc explicitly TODOs this at `02-logging.md:186` — it requires a notification driver, a target queue, and a sink; out of scope here. This issue covers operational logs only.
- Distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry / Jaeger / `osprofiler`). Listed as a separate gap in `project_forge_known_gaps`; spans across services are unrelated to in-pod log emission.
- Per-namespace log routing or multi-tenant log isolation. These are aggregator-side concerns.
- Adding a sidecar log shipper to the Keystone Pod. The 12-Factor posture (stdout/stderr) is sufficient; a per-pod sidecar would duplicate what a node-level Fluent Bit DaemonSet already does.
- Backporting the new field to a CRD conversion webhook. `v1alpha1` clients without `spec.logging` continue to work — the defaulting webhook materializes the field at admission, mirroring the `Resources`/`UWSGI` precedent.

**References**:
- Current state: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go:41-82` (no `oslo.log` keys), `reconcile_deployment.go:317-351` (no uWSGI logging flags), absence of any `logging.conf` / `log_file` / `use_stderr` reference in `operators/keystone/` and `images/keystone/`
- Defaulting precedent: `Resources`/`Bootstrap.AdminUser` defaulting at `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go:80-132`; `UWSGISpec` shape at `keystone_types.go:267-309`
- 12-Factor logging posture (target state): `architecture/docs/06-operations/02-observability/02-logging.md:5,37-65`
- Aggregation pipeline plan (out of scope here, but consumer of this work): `architecture/docs/06-operations/02-observability/02-logging.md:87-168`
- Operator-log guidance (do not conflate with this issue — controller-runtime logs are unaffected): `docs/guides/observability.md:145-160`
- Related: CC-0080 (env-var overrides), CC-0077 (immutable ConfigMap retention), CC-0084 (graceful termination — this issue reuses the same uWSGI command path)

**Labels:** enhancement


**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/297

stories.title: Operator sees Keystone application logs via kubectl logs out-of-the-box (CC-0098)
stories.want: freshly applied Keystone CRs to emit oslo.log lines (auth events, token validation, federation errors, tracebacks) on the pod's stderr without any explicit spec.logging block
stories.so_that: I can debug auth failures and federation issues with `kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone` instead of `kubectl exec`-ing in to reconfigure logging by hand
criteria: When `spec.logging` is omitted, the rendered keystone.conf [DEFAULT] contains use_stderr=true
The defaulting webhook materializes `&LoggingSpec{Format:"text", Level:"INFO", Debug:false}` so downstream code never sees a nil pointer (REQ-001)
The Keystone container's stderr stream contains oslo.log-format lines matching `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.mmm <pid> <LEVEL> keystone.<module> [<req-id>] <message>` interleaved with uWSGI access lines
uWSGI access lines are visually distinguishable from oslo.log lines because uwsgiCommand passes `--log-master` and an explicit `--log-format` (REQ-006)
No new Service, Mount, or sidecar is introduced — the existing `<name>-config` ConfigMap mount carries everything (REQ-004)

stories.title: Aggregator-pipeline owner enables structured JSON logs via a single CRD switch (CC-0098)
stories.role: observability engineer (consumer of the planned Fluent Bit → Loki/OpenSearch pipeline)
stories.want: to flip Keystone over to one-JSON-object-per-line oslo.log records by patching `spec.logging.format: json`
stories.so_that: the on-pod source emits records that the future log-shipper can ingest into Loki/OpenSearch without per-line regex parsing, and `kubectl logs … | jq -e .` succeeds on every line
criteria: Setting `spec.logging.format: json` causes reconcileConfig to render a sibling `logging.conf` into the immutable `<name>-config-<hash>` ConfigMap (REQ-004)
The rendered logging.conf wires `oslo_log.formatters.JSONFormatter` to a stderr StreamHandler at the configured root level
keystone.conf [DEFAULT] gains `log_config_append=/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/logging.conf` only on the JSON path
Switching format from text → json produces a new ConfigMap (different content hash) and a new Deployment ReplicaSet (config-hash annotation rolled, CC-0077)
After the rollout the new pod's stderr passes `kubectl logs … | jq -e .` per line — verified by an e2e step
Switching back to text removes `logging.conf` from the next ConfigMap revision and removes `log_config_append` from keystone.conf

stories.title: Operator raises log verbosity for one Keystone CR without ConfigMap surgery (CC-0098)
stories.role: platform operator triaging a live LDAP/federation incident
stories.want: to flip `spec.logging.debug: true` and selectively raise per-logger levels with `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels` for the affected CR only
stories.so_that: I can capture DEBUG-level traces for the running deployment without editing the immutable `<name>-config-<hash>` ConfigMap or restarting the operator
criteria: Setting `spec.logging.debug: true` adds `debug=true` to keystone.conf [DEFAULT] (REQ-003)

Setting `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels: {"keystone.middleware":"DEBUG","sqlalchemy.engine":"WARNING"}` produces `default_log_levels=keystone.middleware=DEBUG,sqlalchemy.engine=WARNING` in [DEFAULT] in deterministic key order (REQ-005)
Each spec.logging change rolls a new `<name>-config-<hash>` ConfigMap and triggers a rolling Deployment update via the existing config-hash annotation (no separate condition added, CC-0077)
Reverting to `spec.logging.debug: false` rolls the ConfigMap back and the resulting pod stops emitting DEBUG lines

stories.title: Cluster admin gets fast, field-level rejection for invalid logging config (CC-0098)
stories.want: the admission webhook to reject malformed `spec.logging` (unknown enum, bad per-logger level) at apply time with field paths
stories.so_that: typos surface immediately at the kubectl call site instead of producing a half-rolled-out Keystone with broken oslo.log
criteria: `spec.logging.format` outside `{text, json}` is rejected by both the CRD enum and the validating webhook (defense in depth, mirrors UWSGISpec pattern)
`spec.logging.level` outside `{DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL}` is rejected by both the CRD enum and the validating webhook
`spec.logging.perLoggerLevels` value not in the level enum is rejected with a `field.Invalid` error at `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels[<key>]` (REQ-007)
Empty string keys in `perLoggerLevels` are rejected with `field.Invalid` at `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels` (REQ-007)
The validation runs on both create and update paths via the existing `KeystoneWebhook.validate()` central function (no parallel path)

stories.title: Documentation reader finds the application-log access path and the JSON switch (CC-0098)
stories.want: the user-facing observability guide and CRD reference to document `spec.logging`, `kubectl logs` access, and the JSON formatter
stories.so_that: I can adopt the feature without reading the Go source and link the architecture aggregation pipeline to its on-pod prerequisite
criteria: `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md` adds a `LoggingSpec` row to the spec table and a narrative section covering Format, Level, Debug, PerLoggerLevels with the level enum

`docs/guides/observability.md` adds a *Keystone application logs* section ABOVE the existing *Controller logs (last resort)* section at line ~145, including a `kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone` example, the difference between uWSGI access lines and oslo.log lines, and how to flip to `format: json`
`docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md` notes that the config sub-reconciler now renders an optional `logging.conf` and adds `[DEFAULT] use_stderr/debug/default_log_levels`

`architecture/docs/06-operations/02-observability/02-logging.md` (read-only architecture reference) gets a cross-link from the aggregation-pipeline section to the new `spec.logging.format: json` path as the on-pod prerequisite — the CADF audit-logging TODO at :186 is left untouched (out of scope)
All four doc files reference the feature ID CC-0098 in the title-line `feature:` frontmatter or the inline cross-reference

requirements.description: The system SHALL expose `Logging *LoggingSpec` as an optional pointer field on `KeystoneSpec`, and the defaulting webhook SHALL materialize `&LoggingSpec{Format:"text", Level:"INFO", Debug:false}` whenever the pointer is nil (CC-0098, mirrors Resources/Bootstrap defaulting).
requirements.rationale: A nil `Logging` pointer must never reach the reconciler — REQ-002 requires the webhook to be the single source of truth for the production baseline (use_stderr=true, format=text, INFO). Relying on `+kubebuilder:default` markers alone is insufficient because the markers do not fire on the omitted-parent path.

scenarios.name: Nil Logging materialized to defaults
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is admitted with no `spec.logging` block
scenarios.then: the mutating webhook sets `obj.Spec.Logging = &LoggingSpec{Format:"text", Level:"INFO", Debug:false, PerLoggerLevels:nil}` before persistence
and_then: downstream reconciler code dereferences `keystone.Spec.Logging` without a nil check
the persisted object stored in etcd contains the materialized struct so `kubectl get keystone -o yaml` reflects the effective values

scenarios.name: Explicit Logging is preserved
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is admitted with `spec.logging: {format: json, level: DEBUG, debug: true, perLoggerLevels: {...}}`
scenarios.then: the mutating webhook leaves all four fields untouched
and_then: no zero-value defaulting overwrites the explicit `debug: true` (analogous to the existing UWSGISpec.HTTPKeepAlive defaulting carve-out at keystone_webhook.go:99-106)

scenarios.name: Partial Logging is completed
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is admitted with `spec.logging: {format: json}` and Level/Debug omitted
scenarios.then: the mutating webhook fills Level="INFO" and Debug=false but leaves Format="json" and PerLoggerLevels untouched
and_then: the partial-vs-full path is covered by a dedicated test mirroring TestDefault_UWSGIDefaultsProcessesAndThreadsOnly

requirements.description: The validating webhook SHALL reject `spec.logging.format`/`spec.logging.level` values outside the documented enums and SHALL reject `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels` map values outside `{DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL}` and empty-string map keys, returning `field.Invalid`/`field.NotSupported` errors at the precise field path (CC-0098, defense-in-depth alongside CRD enum markers).
requirements.rationale: Catches typos and invalid level strings at the kubectl call site — a malformed perLoggerLevels value would otherwise produce a `logging.conf` that oslo.log refuses to load, leaving the pod with no application logs and no clear failure surface.

scenarios.name: Unknown format rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is admitted with `spec.logging.format: yaml`
scenarios.then: the validating webhook returns `field.NotSupported` (or equivalent) at `spec.logging.format`
and_then: the request fails admission and the object is not persisted
the CRD enum marker provides a duplicate first-line check (defense-in-depth pattern from REQ-007 of CC-0011)

scenarios.name: Unknown level rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is admitted with `spec.logging.level: TRACE`
scenarios.then: the validating webhook returns `field.NotSupported` at `spec.logging.level`

scenarios.name: Invalid per-logger level rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is admitted with `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels: {keystone.middleware: VERBOSE}`
scenarios.then: the validating webhook returns `field.Invalid` at `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels[keystone.middleware]` with a message naming the allowed enum
and_then: no partial config is applied — the entire admission fails atomically

scenarios.name: Empty per-logger key rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is admitted with `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels: {"": DEBUG}`
scenarios.then: the validating webhook returns `field.Invalid` at `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels` with message "logger name must not be empty"

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL extend keystone.conf [DEFAULT] with `use_stderr=true`, `debug=<spec.logging.debug>`, and (when non-empty) `default_log_levels=<rendered from PerLoggerLevels>`, regardless of the chosen format (CC-0098, reconcile_config.go:41-82).
requirements.rationale: `use_stderr=true` is the only knob that prevents oslo.log from falling back to its built-in file path which is not created in the container — disabling it would re-introduce the silent observability hole. `debug=true` is independent of the root level because oslo.log gates several extra-verbose code paths (SQL echo, auth backend tracing) on the debug flag specifically.

scenarios.name: Default text path emits use_stderr=true and debug=false
scenarios.when: reconcileConfig runs with the webhook-defaulted LoggingSpec (format=text, level=INFO, debug=false)
scenarios.then: the rendered keystone.conf [DEFAULT] section contains `use_stderr=true` and `debug=false`
and_then: no `default_log_levels` key is emitted because PerLoggerLevels is empty
no `log_config_append` key is emitted because format=text

scenarios.name: Debug toggle propagates
scenarios.when: reconcileConfig runs with `spec.logging.debug: true`
scenarios.then: the rendered keystone.conf [DEFAULT] contains `debug=true`
and_then: the resulting ConfigMap content hash differs from the debug=false revision, rolling the Deployment via the existing config-hash annotation

scenarios.name: use_stderr is non-overridable by ExtraConfig conflicts that would re-introduce the bug
scenarios.when: a CR sets both `spec.logging` and `spec.extraConfig: {DEFAULT: {use_stderr: false}}`
scenarios.then: the rendered keystone.conf carries the user-supplied `use_stderr=false` (ExtraConfig wins per the existing MergeDefaults precedence at reconcile_config.go:103-105) — but the controller emits a Warning event `LoggingStderrDisabled` so the operator is aware the application-log path is intentionally broken
and_then: the warning event reason is captured in keystone-events.md
the test asserts both the rendered key and the emitted Warning event

requirements.description: When `spec.logging.format == "json"`, the reconciler SHALL render a sibling `logging.conf` into the same immutable `<name>-config-<hash>` ConfigMap that wires `oslo_log.formatters.JSONFormatter` to a stderr StreamHandler at the configured root Level, AND SHALL add `log_config_append=/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/logging.conf` to keystone.conf [DEFAULT]. When format is `text`, the ConfigMap SHALL NOT contain a `logging.conf` entry and `log_config_append` SHALL NOT be emitted (CC-0098).
requirements.rationale: The text path keeps the current ConfigMap shape (no churn) and relies on oslo.log's built-in line format. The JSON path is the on-pod prerequisite for the planned Fluent Bit → Loki/OpenSearch aggregation pipeline (architecture 02-logging.md:87-168).

scenarios.name: Text path produces no logging.conf
scenarios.when: the LoggingSpec materializes with format=text
scenarios.then: the immutable ConfigMap data map contains keys `keystone.conf` and `api-paste.ini` only
and_then: no `logging.conf` key exists
keystone.conf [DEFAULT] does not contain `log_config_append`

scenarios.name: JSON path renders logging.conf and log_config_append
scenarios.when: the LoggingSpec is `{format: json, level: INFO, debug: false}`
scenarios.then: the ConfigMap data map contains key `logging.conf` whose contents include `[loggers]`, `[handlers]`, `[formatters]`, a `[logger_root] level = INFO`, `[handler_stderr] class = StreamHandler` and `args = (sys.stderr,)`, and `[formatter_json] class = oslo_log.formatters.JSONFormatter`
and_then: keystone.conf [DEFAULT] contains `log_config_append=/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/logging.conf`
no new VolumeMount is added — the same `<name>-config` mount at /etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/ carries the new file

scenarios.name: Switching from json → text removes logging.conf in the next revision
scenarios.when: an existing CR is patched from `spec.logging.format: json` to `spec.logging.format: text`
scenarios.then: the next reconcile creates a new immutable ConfigMap with a different hash that has no `logging.conf` entry and no `log_config_append` in keystone.conf
and_then: the previous ConfigMap is retained per CC-0077 retention policy and pruned later — not deleted by this reconciler

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL render `spec.logging.perLoggerLevels` into a single `default_log_levels=<logger>=<level>,<logger>=<level>,…` value in keystone.conf [DEFAULT] using deterministic key ordering (sorted alphabetically by logger name) so the ConfigMap content hash is stable across reconciles for unchanged input (CC-0098).
requirements.rationale: An unstable rendering would churn the `<name>-config-<hash>` ConfigMap on every reconcile, defeating the immutable-ConfigMap retention scheme (CC-0077) and triggering pointless rolling updates.

scenarios.name: Empty PerLoggerLevels omits the key
scenarios.when: spec.logging.perLoggerLevels is nil or empty
scenarios.then: no `default_log_levels` key is emitted in [DEFAULT]

scenarios.name: Multi-entry rendering is deterministic
scenarios.when: spec.logging.perLoggerLevels is `{keystone.middleware: DEBUG, sqlalchemy.engine: WARNING}`
scenarios.then: [DEFAULT] contains exactly `default_log_levels=keystone.middleware=DEBUG,sqlalchemy.engine=WARNING` (sorted alphabetically)
and_then: two reconciles in a row over the same CR produce byte-identical keystone.conf and therefore byte-identical ConfigMap content hashes

scenarios.name: Reverse-iteration safety
scenarios.when: the same map is provided as `{sqlalchemy.engine: WARNING, keystone.middleware: DEBUG}` (different Go map iteration order)
scenarios.then: the rendered value is identical to the previous scenario — `keystone.middleware=DEBUG,sqlalchemy.engine=WARNING`

requirements.description: The `uwsgiCommand` builder SHALL append `--log-master` and an explicit `--log-format` argument so uWSGI's own request access lines remain syntactically distinguishable from oslo.log application records on stderr, regardless of LoggingSpec.Format (CC-0098, reconcile_deployment.go:317-351).
requirements.rationale: Without `--log-master`, uWSGI worker stderr can be intermittently swallowed under heavy load. The explicit `--log-format` makes the access line shape stable and lets downstream tooling tell the two record kinds apart on the message field — important for both the text path (visual inspection) and the JSON path (where oslo.log records are JSON but uWSGI lines remain plain text with a fixed pattern).

scenarios.name: Default invocation includes the new flags
scenarios.when: uwsgiCommand is called with the webhook-defaulted UWSGISpec
scenarios.then: the rendered argv contains `--log-master` and `--log-format "%(method) %(uri) => generated %(rsize) bytes in %(msecs) msecs (%(proto) %(status))"` between the existing `--http-keepalive` and `--wsgi-file` flags
and_then: TestUwsgiCommand_FlagOrderDeterministic continues to pass — the new flags slot in at a fixed index

scenarios.name: Flags emitted regardless of httpKeepAlive
scenarios.when: uwsgiCommand is called with httpKeepAlive=false
scenarios.then: the rendered argv still contains `--log-master` and `--log-format <…>`

scenarios.name: Flags emitted regardless of LoggingSpec.Format
scenarios.when: uwsgiCommand is called for a Keystone CR with format=json (i.e. the deployment reconciler invokes uwsgiCommand identically in both paths)
scenarios.then: the rendered argv is byte-identical to the format=text invocation
and_then: the LoggingSpec is intentionally not threaded into uwsgiCommand — uWSGI logging shape is independent of oslo.log format

requirements.description: Any change to `spec.logging` (Format, Level, Debug, PerLoggerLevels) SHALL produce a different `<name>-config-<hash>` ConfigMap and trigger a Deployment rolling update via the existing config-hash annotation; no new condition is added to KeystoneStatus (CC-0098, reuses CC-0077 immutable-ConfigMap + config-hash mechanism).
requirements.rationale: Logging is a config-only concern; the reconciler already surfaces config readiness via the `ConfigReady` condition. Adding a new condition would duplicate state without adding signal. The immutable ConfigMap + content-hash flow is the documented rollout mechanism (CC-0077).

scenarios.name: debug toggle rolls the ConfigMap and Deployment
scenarios.when: a CR is patched from `spec.logging.debug: false` to `spec.logging.debug: true`
scenarios.then: the next reconcile creates a new ConfigMap with a different content hash AND updates the Deployment's pod template config-hash annotation
and_then: a new ReplicaSet is created and the previous one is scaled down per the existing rollout strategy

scenarios.name: format toggle rolls the ConfigMap and Deployment
scenarios.when: a CR is patched from `spec.logging.format: text` to `spec.logging.format: json`
scenarios.then: the next reconcile creates a new ConfigMap whose data map gains a `logging.conf` key and whose keystone.conf gains `log_config_append=…`
and_then: the Deployment's config-hash annotation is updated and a rolling update is triggered

scenarios.name: No-op reconcile is a no-op
scenarios.when: two consecutive reconciles run with no spec change
scenarios.then: the second reconcile observes the same ConfigMap name and does NOT update the Deployment annotation
and_then: this is verified by an envtest integration test that asserts ResourceVersion of the Deployment is unchanged

requirements.description: The CRD manifests in both `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` AND `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` SHALL be regenerated and committed in the same PR as the API type change, including the LoggingSpec schema with enum constraints on `format` and `level` (CC-0098).
requirements.rationale: Out-of-band schemas confuse Helm upgraders and lead to admission rejections in production. The regenerate-and-commit invariant is enforced by the same `make manifests` flow that landed the UWSGISpec, AutoscalingSpec, and NetworkPolicySpec changes.

scenarios.name: Both CRD files contain the new schema
scenarios.when: `make manifests` is run after the LoggingSpec is added to keystone_types.go
scenarios.then: both CRD YAML files gain a `logging` property under `spec.properties` with `format` (enum text;json), `level` (enum DEBUG;INFO;WARNING;ERROR;CRITICAL), `debug` (boolean), `perLoggerLevels` (additionalProperties string)
and_then: no other unrelated fields in the CRD diff
`go test ./operators/keystone/...` passes

scenarios.name: Helm and config CRDs stay in sync
scenarios.when: diffing the two CRD files
scenarios.then: the LoggingSpec section is byte-identical between the two files (modulo the standard helm/conversion-webhook header)

scenarios.name: CI surfaces drift
scenarios.when: a developer hand-edits keystone_types.go without running `make manifests`
scenarios.then: the existing schema-drift CI check (or `make manifests && git diff --exit-code`) fails

requirements.description: End-to-end: a freshly applied Keystone CR with no `spec.logging` block SHALL produce oslo.log-format application lines on the pod's stderr within 60 seconds of reaching Ready=True, and patching `spec.logging.format: json` SHALL result in the new pod emitting parseable JSON-per-line records on stderr (CC-0098).
requirements.rationale: Unit tests cannot prove the in-container oslo.log integration works — the rendered keystone.conf must actually be loaded by the Keystone Python process. A chainsaw e2e test against a kind cluster catches integration regressions (e.g. the wrong oslo.log key name, a typo in logging.conf).

scenarios.name: Default path emits oslo.log lines
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR with no spec.logging is applied and reaches Ready=True
scenarios.then: `kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone --tail=200` returns at least one line matching `^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\.\d{3} \d+ (DEBUG|INFO|WARNING|ERROR|CRITICAL) keystone\.`
and_then: the assertion runs within a 60-second timeout
the test also asserts a uWSGI access line is present so we know both streams reach stderr

scenarios.name: JSON path produces parseable JSON
scenarios.when: the CR is patched to `spec.logging.format: json` and the rolling update completes
scenarios.then: every non-empty line of `kubectl logs … --tail=200` from the new pod parses as JSON via `jq -e .` for oslo.log records (uWSGI access lines are excluded by selector or grep)
and_then: each JSON object contains keys `name`, `levelname`, `message` (oslo_log.formatters.JSONFormatter contract)

scenarios.name: Debug toggle rolls a new ReplicaSet
scenarios.when: the CR is patched to `spec.logging.debug: true`
scenarios.then: a new immutable `<name>-config-<hash>` ConfigMap appears AND a new ReplicaSet is created (resource version of the Deployment.spec.template.annotations["keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/config-hash"] changes)
and_then: the new pod emits at least one `DEBUG` oslo.log line from a `keystone.*` logger

requirements.description: Documentation SHALL be updated in lock-step with the implementation: keystone-crd.md (Reference, new field), observability.md (How-to, application-log access path with JSON variant), keystone-reconciler.md (Reference, mention logging.conf), and architecture/02-logging.md (cross-link only, read-only file kept under architecture/) (CC-0098).
requirements.rationale: An undocumented CRD field is invisible — operators reading docs would still resort to ConfigMap edits. The Reference + How-to split follows the existing Diátaxis convention used for CC-0084, CC-0094, etc.

scenarios.name: Reference doc covers all four LoggingSpec fields
scenarios.when: a reader opens docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md
scenarios.then: the spec table contains a `logging` row pointing to a narrative section that documents Format (with enum), Level (with enum), Debug, and PerLoggerLevels (with map shape)
and_then: the narrative includes a YAML example of `spec.logging: {format: json, level: DEBUG, perLoggerLevels: {keystone.middleware: DEBUG}}`
the section frontmatter `feature:` line includes `CC-0098`

scenarios.name: How-to guide section appears above Controller logs
scenarios.when: a reader opens docs/guides/observability.md
scenarios.then: a new `## Keystone application logs` section is positioned above the existing `## Controller logs (last resort)` section at line ~145
and_then: the section contains a `kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone --tail=200 -f` example, an explanation of uWSGI vs oslo.log lines, and a `kubectl patch keystone … --type=merge -p '{"spec":{"logging":{"format":"json"}}}'` example with `kubectl logs … | jq -e .` verification

scenarios.name: Reconciler reference notes new ConfigMap entry
scenarios.when: a reader opens docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md
scenarios.then: the config sub-reconciler entry mentions that `logging.conf` is rendered when `spec.logging.format == json` and that [DEFAULT] gains `use_stderr/debug/default_log_levels`

scenarios.name: Architecture cross-link added in read-only file
scenarios.when: a reader opens architecture/docs/06-operations/02-observability/02-logging.md
scenarios.then: the aggregation-pipeline section gains a one-sentence cross-link to `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md#logging` describing `format: json` as the on-pod prerequisite
and_then: the CADF audit-logging TODO at line :186 is left untouched (out of scope)
this is the ONLY edit to anything under architecture/ — that tree is otherwise treated as read-only reference material

tasks.title: Add LoggingSpec struct and Logging *LoggingSpec field to KeystoneSpec in operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go (CC-0098, REQ-001) — kubebuilder enum/default markers on Format/Level/Debug, +optional on PerLoggerLevels.
Run `make generate` to refresh zz_generated.deepcopy.go for the new LoggingSpec; verify `go build ./operators/keystone/...` compiles (CC-0098, REQ-001).

Materialize LoggingSpec defaults in KeystoneWebhook.Default() following the Resources-when-nil pattern at keystone_webhook.go:119-130; partial-fill zero values when the pointer is non-nil (CC-0098, REQ-001).

Add unit tests TestDefault_LoggingNilMaterializesDefaultSpec / _PreservesExplicitValues / _PartialFillsZeroValuesOnly to keystone_webhook_test.go using the existing reconcileConfig keystone helper (CC-0098, REQ-001).

Extend KeystoneWebhook.validate() with spec.logging branch: enum check on Format/Level (defense-in-depth), per-key Invalid for unknown PerLoggerLevels values, and Invalid for empty-string map keys; place the block alongside the UWSGI validation at keystone_webhook.go:290 (CC-0098, REQ-002).

Add unit tests TestValidate_LoggingRejectsUnknownFormat / _RejectsUnknownLevel / _PerLoggerLevelsRejectsUnknownLevel / _PerLoggerLevelsRejectsEmptyKey to keystone_webhook_test.go asserting the field paths returned (CC-0098, REQ-002).

Extend reconcileConfig defaults map with use_stderr=true, debug=<spec.logging.debug>, and (when non-empty) default_log_levels=<sorted PerLoggerLevels> in keystone.conf [DEFAULT] (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go:41-82, CC-0098, REQ-003, REQ-005).

Add a renderLoggingConf helper that builds the four-section logging.conf INI string from LoggingSpec.Level (oslo_log.formatters.JSONFormatter wired to a stderr StreamHandler); wire it into reconcileConfig only when format=json AND add log_config_append=/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/logging.conf to [DEFAULT] (CC-0098, REQ-004).

Emit a Warning event with reason=LoggingStderrDisabled from reconcileConfig when the merged [DEFAULT].use_stderr is not "true" after extraConfig merge — surfaces the CC-0098 REQ-003 corner case where ExtraConfig overrides the safe default (CC-0098, REQ-003).

Add unit tests TestReconcileConfig_LoggingDefaultsEmitUseStderr / _LoggingDebugTruePropagates / _LoggingExtraConfigUseStderrFalseEmitsWarningEvent to reconcile_config_test.go using the existing FakeRecorder pattern (CC-0098, REQ-003).

Add unit tests TestReconcileConfig_LoggingTextPathOmitsLoggingConf / _LoggingJSONPathRendersLoggingConfAndAppend / _LoggingJSONToTextDropsLoggingConf to reconcile_config_test.go asserting both ConfigMap data keys and ConfigMap-name change across the toggle (CC-0098, REQ-004).

Add unit tests TestReconcileConfig_LoggingPerLoggerLevelsDeterministicOrder / _LoggingPerLoggerLevelsEmptyOmitsKey to reconcile_config_test.go covering deterministic alphabetical rendering and the empty-map omission (CC-0098, REQ-005).

Append --log-master and --log-format "%(method) %(uri) => generated %(rsize) bytes in %(msecs) msecs (%(proto) %(status))" to uwsgiCommand at a stable index between --http-keepalive and --wsgi-file (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:317-351, CC-0098, REQ-006).
Add unit tests TestUwsgiCommand_IncludesLogMasterAndLogFormat and update TestUwsgiCommand_FlagOrderDeterministic to assert the new flag pair index in reconcile_deployment_test.go (CC-0098, REQ-006).

Add envtest integration tests TestIntegration_LoggingDebugTogglesRollsConfigMapAndDeployment / _NoOpReconcileDoesNotRollDeployment to operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go using the existing scaffolding (CC-0098, REQ-007).

Run `make manifests` to regenerate operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml AND operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml; verify both files contain the spec.logging schema with both enum constraints (CC-0098, REQ-008).

Add envtest test TestIntegration_CRDSchemaContainsLoggingSpec to operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/integration_test.go that loads the CRD from config/crd/bases and asserts the openAPI schema for spec.logging (CC-0098, REQ-008).

Create tests/e2e/keystone/logging/00-keystone-cr.yaml (default spec.logging omitted), 01-patch-debug-true.yaml, 02-patch-format-json.yaml, 03-patch-invalid-perloggerlevel.yaml as siblings to the existing tests/e2e/keystone/uwsgi/ fixture (CC-0098, REQ-009).

Create tests/e2e/keystone/logging/chainsaw-test.yaml with four steps: (a) assert oslo.log line in `kubectl logs` within 60s of Ready=True, (b) debug toggle rolls a new ReplicaSet and emits DEBUG line, (c) format=json yields jq-parseable records, (d) invalid perLoggerLevels rejected at admission. Mirror the catch/podLogs/events scaffolding from the uwsgi chainsaw test (CC-0098, REQ-002, REQ-009).

Update docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md: add CC-0098 to the title-line `feature:` list, add a `logging` row to the spec table, and add a narrative `### LoggingSpec` section documenting Format/Level/Debug/PerLoggerLevels with a YAML example (CC-0098, REQ-010).

Update docs/guides/observability.md: add a `## Keystone application logs` section above the existing `## Controller logs (last resort)` at line ~145 with `kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone` example, uWSGI-vs-oslo.log explanation, and a `kubectl patch … logging.format=json` + `jq -e .` verification snippet (CC-0098, REQ-010).

Update docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md: add a sentence to the config sub-reconciler entry noting that an optional `logging.conf` is rendered when `spec.logging.format == json` and that [DEFAULT] gains use_stderr/debug/default_log_levels (CC-0098, REQ-010).

Add a single one-sentence cross-link to architecture/docs/06-operations/02-observability/02-logging.md pointing readers from the aggregation-pipeline section to docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md#loggingspec as the on-pod prerequisite for `format: json` ingestion. Leave the CADF audit-logging TODO at line :186 untouched. This is the ONLY edit to anything under architecture/ in this feature (CC-0098, REQ-010).

review_criteria: BLOCKER: All SHALL requirements REQ-001…REQ-010 have at least one passing unit/integration/e2e test (mapped via test_specifications.requirement_id)
BLOCKER: Both CRD YAML files (helm/keystone-operator/crds and config/crd/bases) regenerated and committed; `make manifests && git diff --exit-code` is clean
BLOCKER: `go test ./operators/keystone/...` passes; the new chainsaw suite tests/e2e/keystone/logging/ passes on a kind cluster
BLOCKER: No new Service, Mount, or sidecar introduced — the existing <name>-config ConfigMap mount carries logging.conf (REQ-004 architecture invariant)
BLOCKER: KeystoneStatus has no new condition — logging readiness is surfaced via the existing ConfigReady condition (REQ-007)
CRITICAL: PerLoggerLevels rendering is deterministic (alphabetical) — the same map produces byte-identical keystone.conf across reconciles (REQ-005); covered by an explicit two-reconcile unit test
CRITICAL: uwsgiCommand changes are LoggingSpec-independent (REQ-006) — no LoggingSpec is threaded into uwsgiCommand; the new flags appear in both format=text and format=json paths
CRITICAL: Defense-in-depth validation matches the project pattern — both CRD enum markers AND validate() webhook checks are present for Format/Level (mirrors UWSGISpec REQ-003 of CC-0040)
CRITICAL: ExtraConfig precedence is preserved — when extraConfig sets DEFAULT.use_stderr=false, the override wins AND a Warning event LoggingStderrDisabled is emitted (REQ-003 corner case)
MINOR: All four documentation files updated and reference CC-0098 in their feature frontmatter (REQ-010)
MINOR: Test files are tagged in the test_specifications expected field with [unit]/[integration]/[e2e] so reviewers can verify tier coverage at a glance
MINOR: No edits to anything under architecture/ except the single cross-link sentence in 02-logging.md — that tree is otherwise read-only reference material per the documented convention
implementation_notes:
Architectural decisions:

1. LoggingSpec is an OPTIONAL POINTER (Logging *LoggingSpec) on KeystoneSpec, mirroring UWSGISpec/AutoscalingSpec/NetworkPolicySpec. The defaulting webhook materializes the struct on nil so all downstream code dereferences without nil checks (REQ-001). This matches the documented Resources/Bootstrap defaulting precedent at keystone_webhook.go:80-132 and avoids the +kubebuilder:default trap that REQ-002 of CC-0040 already documented.

2. The webhook is the single source of truth for defaults. Although Format/Level/Debug also carry +kubebuilder:default markers (defense-in-depth), the markers DO NOT fire on the omitted-parent path — only the webhook does. This is why TestDefault_LoggingNilMaterializesDefaultSpec is non-redundant.

3. Validation is layered (defense-in-depth): the CRD enum markers reject most malformed values at the API server's openAPI schema layer; the validate() webhook re-checks for clearer field-level errors and to cover map-value validation (perLoggerLevels values cannot be expressed as a CRD enum on additionalProperties).

4. PerLoggerLevels is rendered into a SINGLE INI key default_log_levels=<a>=<lvl>,<b>=<lvl>… in alphabetical order to keep the ConfigMap content hash stable across reconciles. Go map iteration is non-deterministic — without explicit sorting we would churn the immutable ConfigMap on every reconcile, defeating CC-0077 retention.

5. logging.conf is ONLY rendered when format=json. The text path keeps the current ConfigMap shape (zero churn for existing CRs that do not opt in). On a json→text downgrade, the next ConfigMap revision drops the logging.conf key entirely.

6. uwsgiCommand changes (REQ-006) are LoggingSpec-INDEPENDENT. The --log-master/--log-format flags improve uWSGI's own access-line discipline regardless of oslo.log format. Threading LoggingSpec into uwsgiCommand would conflate two concerns and break TestUwsgiCommand_FlagOrderDeterministic.

7. ExtraConfig precedence is preserved (existing MergeDefaults at reconcile_config.go:103-105). When a user explicitly sets DEFAULT.use_stderr=false via extraConfig the override wins — that is the documented contract — but the controller emits a LoggingStderrDisabled Warning event so the operator notices that they have re-introduced the silent-observability-hole symptom on purpose. Tested via FakeRecorder.

8. No new KeystoneStatus condition (REQ-007). Logging is purely a config concern; ConfigReady already surfaces config readiness. Adding a new condition would duplicate state without adding signal.

9. architecture/ tree is treated as READ-ONLY reference. The ONLY edit there is a single cross-link sentence in 02-logging.md (REQ-010). All implementation, test, and user-facing docs live under operators/, tests/, and docs/.

Data model: LoggingSpec carries 4 fields (Format string, Level string, Debug bool, PerLoggerLevels map[string]string). Stored on KeystoneSpec.Logging (pointer). DeepCopy is generated by `make generate`. No persistence beyond the Keystone CR — no separate Secret/ConfigMap is introduced (the rendered logging.conf rides in the existing immutable <name>-config-<hash> ConfigMap).

Architecture diagram (component relationships):

```
  KeystoneSpec.Logging (CRD field, pointer)
        │
        │  (1) admission: defaulting webhook materializes nil → defaults
        │      validating webhook rejects bad enums and bad per-logger levels
        ▼
  KeystoneWebhook.Default() / .validate()  ──── operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go
        │
        │  (2) reconcile: read spec.Logging from etcd-backed CR
        ▼
  KeystoneReconciler.reconcileConfig()  ──────── operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go
        │      │
        │      ├─► [DEFAULT] use_stderr/debug/default_log_levels  (always)
        │      ├─► [DEFAULT] log_config_append=…/logging.conf      (json only)
        │      └─► logging.conf  (json only, sibling of keystone.conf)
        │
        │  (3) immutable ConfigMap with content-hash suffix
        ▼
  ConfigMap <name>-config-<hash>  ─────────────── data: {keystone.conf, api-paste.ini, [logging.conf]}
        │
        │  (4) mounted at /etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/ in the Keystone container
        ▼
  Deployment .spec.template.metadata.annotations["…/config-hash"] = <hash>
        │
        │  (5) annotation change rolls a new ReplicaSet (existing CC-0077 mechanism)
        ▼
  Keystone pod (uwsgiCommand also gains --log-master / --log-format)
        │
        │  (6) oslo.log writes to stderr (use_stderr=true);
        │      uWSGI access lines also reach stderr but with a distinct format
        ▼
  kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone
```

Data flow (state-changing path):

```
  user kubectl patch keystone … '{"spec":{"logging":{"debug":true}}}'
      → API server admission
          → mutating webhook (Default fills any nil sub-fields)
          → validating webhook (rejects bad enum / bad perLogger value)
      → object persisted in etcd
      → controller-runtime triggers reconcile of the Keystone CR
          → reconcileConfig builds new keystone.conf with debug=true
          → CreateImmutableConfigMap returns NEW name with new hash
          → reconcileDeployment sets pod template annotation to new hash
          → kube-apiserver creates new ReplicaSet, scales down old one
      → new pod boots → oslo.log loads keystone.conf → DEBUG lines on stderr
      → kubectl logs reflects new verbosity
```

Risks:
- (R1) Format/Level enum drift between Go consts, kubebuilder markers, and CRD YAML — mitigated by running `make manifests` (task 4.1) and a CRD-schema integration test (task 4.2).
- (R2) Non-deterministic Go map iteration would churn ConfigMap hashes — mitigated by explicit alphabetical sort in renderPerLoggerLevels (task 2.1) and a deterministic-order test (task 2.6).
- (R3) ExtraConfig user override of use_stderr=false silently re-introduces the bug — mitigated by emitting a Warning event LoggingStderrDisabled (task 2.3) and asserting it in a unit test.
- (R4) oslo_log.formatters.JSONFormatter class path may differ between Keystone images (older vs newer oslo.log packages) — caught by the e2e step that runs `jq -e .` on real pod stderr against the image referenced in tests/e2e/keystone/logging/00-keystone-cr.yaml.
- (R5) On a fresh upgrade, existing CRs in etcd lack spec.logging — but the defaulting webhook fires on next mutation, and the reconciler treats nil as the production baseline (use_stderr=true, format=text). No CRD conversion webhook is needed (mirrors the v1alpha1 Resources defaulting precedent).

Files and patterns referenced:
- Defaulting precedent: operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go:99-130 (UWSGI partial fill; Resources nil-or-empty → defaults)
- Defense-in-depth validation precedent: operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go:288-336 (UWSGI Minimum=1 + Range checks)
- Immutable ConfigMap creation: internal/common/config/CreateImmutableConfigMap (used at reconcile_config.go:152)
- Config-hash → Deployment annotation: existing CC-0077 mechanism in reconcile_deployment.go (search for "config-hash")
- Chainsaw test scaffolding: tests/e2e/keystone/uwsgi/chainsaw-test.yaml is the closest sibling pattern — copy the catch/podLogs/events block verbatim into the new logging/chainsaw-test.yaml.
- FakeRecorder pattern for asserting Warning events in unit tests: see existing reconcile_config_test.go event-assert helpers.
